boundary_reset_audit.exact_fit_condition: det[b_(mu_i-i+j)] = h_s^(-r)*s_(mu+(s^r))(h) only when mu_r>=r-1, so every tail Jacobi-Trudi index is nonnegative

exact.bridge_target: prove every binomially weighted Jensen window for the actual Xi/Phi sequence using hypotheses stronger than unweighted Schur positivity and weaker than all-shift signed-Hankel positivity
exact.conditional_tail_translation: det[b_(mu_i-i+j)] = h_s^(-r)*s_(mu+(s^r))(h) only when mu_r>=r-1, so every tail Jacobi-Trudi index is nonnegative
exact.generic_bridge_counterexample: H(z)=exp(z/100)/((1-z)(1-2z)) is strictly Schur-positive, but J_(3,0)(x) has negative discriminant
exact.ordinary_tail: b_k^(s)=h_(s+k)/h_s for k>=0 and b_k^(s)=0 for k<0
exact.rectangle_target: s_((N^m))(h)>0 for every m>=10 and N>=m-1
exact.rejected_rectangle_target: s_((N^m))(h)>0 for every m>=10 and N>=m-1 is false

primary_literature_audit.finding: Schur log-concavity supports formal rectangular difference identities, but numerical positivity after specialization still needs the relevant Schur values to be positive.

primary_literature_audit.does_not_supply: a conversion from deep endpoint minors to binomially weighted Jensen minors
primary_literature_audit.finding: Hadamard products of totally positive Toeplitz matrices are not closed in general; Wagner proves narrower sufficient classes.

primary_literature_audit.does_not_supply: the open rectangles (N^m), which contain 3x2 blocks for m>=3,N>=2
primary_literature_audit.finding: The newer preprint leaves the general Hadamard Jacobi-Trudi conjecture open and proves a 3x2-avoiding ribbon-like case.

primary_literature_audit.does_not_supply: closure under the Xi scale mixture or an endpoint-to-Jensen theorem
primary_literature_audit.finding: Reciprocal-Gamma arithmetic progressions provide genuine multiplier sequences and explain the fixed-scale model.

proof_boundary: This artifact proves the rectangular Toda identity, rejects the ordinary shifted-tail PF shortcut, and gives an exact strictly Schur-positive sequence whose cubic Jensen polynomial is not hyperbolic. Rigorous order-ten data reject the proposed m>=10 endpoint hierarchy at four initial widths. This rejects that route, not the Xi/Phi Jensen bridge, RH, or Lambda<=0.

rows.claim: Use the normalized endpoint specialization and exact rectangular deep-Schur map.
rows.proof_boundary: Imports the exact coordinate and its separately certified order-ten failure.

rows.claim: Rectangular endpoint Schur values form a two-index tau array.
Desnanot-Jacobi gives the rectangular discrete Toda relation.

diagnostics.scope: Finite symbolic specializations audit the index and sign convention; arbitrary-order validity is Desnanot-Jacobi.

rows.proof_boundary: The right side is a difference, so the identity alone has no positivity direction.

rows.claim: The next rectangle order is exactly strict width-log-concavity of the current row; the first required row fails at four widths.
rows.proof_boundary: This restates rather than proves the next-order inequality.

rows.claim: Ordinary subtraction-free cluster positivity cannot be read off from this signed Toda form.
rows.formula: positive square minus positive neighbor product has undetermined sign
rows.proof_boundary: A different zeta-specific invariant could still control the difference.

rows.claim: Resetting a shifted coefficient tail creates a genuine one-sided Schur specialization.
rows.formula: b_k^(s)=h_(s+k)/h_s for k>=0 and b_k^(s)=0 for k<0
rows.proof_boundary: The reset changes negative-index entries and must be audited before any PF claim.

rows.claim: Tail translation agrees with the shifted deep Schur determinant only when the tail shape is itself deep.

diagnostics.exact_fit_condition: det[b_(mu_i-i+j)] = h_s^(-r)*s_(mu+(s^r))(h) only when mu_r>=r-1, so every tail Jacobi-Trudi index is nonnegative

rows.formula: det[b_(mu_i-i+j)] = h_s^(-r)*s_(mu+(s^r))(h) only when mu_r>=r-1, so every tail Jacobi-Trudi index is nonnegative
rows.proof_boundary: It does not extend positivity to shallow tail shapes.

rows.claim: The smallest shallow tail shape already has a nonzero reset defect.
rows.proof_boundary: This rejects the proposed moving-tail PF equivalence independently of the direct order-ten deep failures.

rows.claim: Littlewood-Richardson positivity cannot fill the missing tail boundary because its required straight tail Schurs are not translated deep Schurs.
rows.formula: moving-tail PF_m does not follow from the deep cone by zero-reset translation
rows.proof_boundary: A strip/interior Toeplitz theorem with the original boundary remains conceivable.

rows.claim: An Edrei specialization with a positive exponential component is strictly Schur-positive for every partition.
rows.proof_boundary: This is a generic proof-safety model, not the zeta endpoint sequence.

rows.claim: The Plancherel summand proves strict positivity at every Schur shape in the countermodel.
rows.proof_boundary: The bounded determinant audit checks implementation; the displayed formula is all-order.

rows.claim: The same strictly Schur-positive sequence has a nonhyperbolic cubic Jensen polynomial.

diagnostics.conclusion: one real zero and one nonreal conjugate pair; the cubic Jensen polynomial is not hyperbolic
diagnostics.strict_schur_reason: the Plancherel summand alone is strictly positive for every partition

rows.proof_boundary: This proves unweighted Schur/PF positivity alone cannot be the Jensen bridge.

rows.claim: The audited Toda, Hadamard, Jacobi-Trudi, and multiplier results support formal pieces but do not supply the surviving Xi/Phi Jensen theorem.

rows.finding: Schur log-concavity supports formal rectangular difference identities, but numerical positivity after specialization still needs the relevant Schur values to be positive.

rows.does_not_supply: a conversion from deep endpoint minors to binomially weighted Jensen minors
rows.finding: Hadamard products of totally positive Toeplitz matrices are not closed in general; Wagner proves narrower sufficient classes.

rows.does_not_supply: the open rectangles (N^m), which contain 3x2 blocks for m>=3,N>=2
rows.finding: The newer preprint leaves the general Hadamard Jacobi-Trudi conjecture open and proves a 3x2-avoiding ribbon-like case.

rows.does_not_supply: closure under the Xi scale mixture or an endpoint-to-Jensen theorem
rows.finding: Reciprocal-Gamma arithmetic progressions provide genuine multiplier sequences and explain the fixed-scale model.

rows.proof_boundary: A bounded route audit, not an exhaustive literature claim.

rows.claim: The zeta-specific endpoint rectangular hierarchy fails at its first uncompleted order.
rows.formula: s_((N^10))(h)<0 for N=9,10,11,12; s_((N^m))(h)>0 for every m>=10 and N>=m-1 is false
rows.proof_boundary: Rejects this all-order signed-Hankel route, not RH or Jensen hyperbolicity.

rows.claim: Use Xi/Phi analytic structure weaker than the rejected signed/deep hierarchy to prove every Jensen-window hyperbolicity statement.
rows.formula: prove every binomially weighted Jensen window for the actual Xi/Phi sequence using hypotheses stronger than unweighted Schur positivity and weaker than all-shift signed-Hankel positivity
rows.proof_boundary: The strict-Schur counterexample proves that a generic Schur/PF implication is false.

exact all-order endpoint-to-heat equivalence with a completed base through order nine and the candidate endpoint hierarchy rejected by the order-ten counterexample

status: exact rectangular Toda coordinate, moving-tail zero-boundary obstruction, strict-Schur/Jensen separation, and rejected all-order endpoint hierarchy; the Xi/Phi Jensen bridge remains open
